Cracca, specifically Lupinus, is a genus of flowering plants that belong to the pea family (Fabaceae). The health benefits of Cracca include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and provide antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cracca include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cracca include teas, capsules, and tinctures, often used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
